metadata.name: ario_ant
metadata.description: AR.IO ANT (Arweave Name Token) Program - Metaplex Core NFT-based name tokens with records, controllers, and metadata

docs: AR.IO ANT (Arweave Name Token) Program
Each ANT is a Metaplex Core NFT. Ownership = NFT holder.
Extended state (records, controllers, metadata) stored in PDAs.
Handles:
- ANT initialization (create config + controllers + @ record)
- Record management (set, remove, transfer records)
- Controller management (add, remove)
- Metadata management (name, ticker, description, keywords, logo)
Transfer is handled natively by Metaplex Core (marketplace compatible).
Lazy ownership reconciliation clears controllers on detected ownership change.

instructions.name: add_acl_page
docs: Allocate the next `AclPage` (`page_idx == acl_config.page_count`).
SDK calls this lazily when the most recent page fills up.

instructions.name: add_controller
docs: Add a controller address.
The controller's per-user paginated ACL (ADR-012) is updated
inline — `controller_acl_config` and `controller_acl_page` are
required accounts, so it is impossible to add a controller
without also writing the matching `record_controller` ACL entry.
The SDK preflights `register_acl_config` + `add_acl_page` ixs in
the same tx if the controller has no head/page yet (or every
page is full). The contract is the source of truth for "ANT
controller add ⇒ ACL entry."

accounts.name: controller_acl_config
docs: Pre-existing `AclConfig` for the controller being added.
SDK preflight bundles `register_acl_config` first if missing.

accounts.name: controller_acl_page
docs: Pre-existing `AclPage` (`page_idx == controller_acl_page.page_idx`)
chosen by the SDK as the destination for the new ACL entry.
SDK preflight bundles `add_acl_page` first if every existing
page is at `MAX_ACL_PAGE_ENTRIES`.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Pays for any `AclPage` realloc growth caused by the new entry.

instructions.name: clear_attributes
docs: Clear ArNS-related traits from the asset's Attributes plugin while
preserving the `ANT Program` routing trait.
The complement of `sync_attributes`: where sync writes
`ArNS Name`/`Type`/`Undername Limit` from a live ArnsRecord, clear
removes them — useful when the canonical record is gone (released
name) or no longer points at this asset (after `reassign_name`).
Both scenarios produce stale traits in the plugin that the asset
owner otherwise has no AR.IO-program path to remove (sync would
fail the post-state `record.ant == asset.key()` check).
Authority must currently hold the asset (matching sync's authority
rule — Owner-authority Attributes plugin). Strictly additive: no
existing flow breaks. See ADR-016 / BD-100 for the architectural
context this closes.

accounts.name: asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset whose Attributes plugin will be reset.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays for any rent the inner CPI may charge. Often the same key as
`authority` but kept separate so a sponsor can pay.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal the asset's current MPL Core owner — Owner-authority
plugin requires the holder to sign UpdatePluginV1.

accounts.name: mpl_core_program
docs: Metaplex Core program — pinned to the canonical ID; required for
the inner CPI.

instructions.name: close_acl_config
docs: Close an empty `AclConfig` head (`page_count == 0`). Refunds rent
to `acl_config.user`.

accounts.name: beneficiary
docs: Receives the rent refund. Must equal `acl_config.user` so funds
always flow back to the wallet that owns the relationship.

instructions.name: close_acl_page
docs: Close the most recently allocated `AclPage` when it is empty.
Pages must be closed in LIFO order so indices stay dense.

accounts.name: beneficiary
docs: Receives the rent refund. Must equal `acl_config.user`.

instructions.name: close_orphaned_record_metadata
docs: Permissionlessly close an orphaned `AntRecordMetadata` PDA whose
corresponding `AntRecord` no longer exists. Rent flows to the caller as
a cleanup incentive.
`RemoveRecord` accepts metadata as `Option`, so a forgetful caller can
close the record without closing its sibling metadata, leaving rent
trapped at the orphan PDA. This instruction recovers that rent without
requiring owner/controller authorization — the only precondition is
that the record is gone (closed by Anchor → owner reassigned to the
System Program and data drained).

accounts.name: record
docs: Anchor's `seeds` constraint here verifies the address derives correctly from the
canonical record seeds for `(asset, undername)` — guaranteeing this is the right
PDA regardless of whether the underlying account exists.

accounts.name: record_metadata
docs: Orphaned metadata PDA to close — rent flows to the caller.

instructions.name: finalize_migration
docs: Permanently disable migration imports (main authority only)

instructions.name: import_account
docs: Import a pre-serialized account during migration

instructions.name: initialize
docs: Initialize an ANT's on-chain state (config, controllers, @ record).
Called after minting a Metaplex Core asset.

accounts.name: asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset (NFT). Verified as owned by mpl-core program.

accounts.name: owner
docs: The NFT owner (must be signer and actual NFT holder)

instructions.name: initialize_migration
docs: Initialize migration config singleton for the ANT program

accounts.name: payer
docs: Payer must be the program's upgrade authority. Explicit `Some(...)`
equality rejects revoked upgrade authority (`None`); the previous
`unwrap_or_default()` would have coerced revoked authority to the
System Program pubkey, which is fragile defense-in-depth (audit

instructions.name: migrate_ant
docs: Migrate an ANT's on-chain state to the latest schema version.
Permissionless: anyone can pay to migrate any ANT — this only
upgrades the data layout and never changes user data.
Uses realloc to handle account size changes between versions.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Anyone can pay to migrate any ANT (permissionless)

instructions.name: reconcile
docs: Force ownership reconciliation — clears controllers if NFT ownership changed.
Call this after a marketplace transfer to ensure clean state.
Permissionless: anyone can trigger reconciliation (it only helps the new owner).

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone can call reconcile (permissionless)

instructions.name: record_acl_controller
docs: Record that `acl_config.user` is a controller of `asset`. Verifies
via `AntControllers` that the user is currently listed.

accounts.name: asset
docs: program is enforced via the constraint and the controllers PDA is
bound to the asset's key.

instructions.name: record_acl_owner
docs: Record that `acl_config.user` owns `asset`. Verifies via the MPL
Core asset account that the user really is the current NFT holder.
Caller picks the target page; SDK convention is "first non-full".

accounts.name: asset
docs: `read_asset_owner`. Owner program is checked there as well.

accounts.name: acl_page
docs: Caller-supplied page. Validated against `acl_config` in handler.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays for realloc growth on append; receives refunds the program
chooses to issue (currently none — pages do not shrink on remove).

instructions.name: register_acl_config
docs: Initialize an `AclConfig` head for `user`. Permissionless. Fails at
the `init` constraint if the head already exists.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Anyone can pay to register an ACL config for any user (permissionless).

instructions.name: remove_acl_controller
docs: Remove a stale controller entry. Verifies via `AntControllers` that
`acl_config.user` is *no longer* listed.

accounts.name: asset
docs: program is enforced via the constraint and the controllers PDA is
bound to the asset's key.

instructions.name: remove_acl_owner
docs: Remove a stale owner entry. Verifies via the MPL Core asset that
`acl_config.user` is *no longer* the owner — never removes a live
entry. Uses `swap_remove`; pages stay sparse until next append.

accounts.name: asset
docs: `read_asset_owner`. Owner program is checked there as well.

accounts.name: acl_page
docs: Caller-supplied page. Validated against `acl_config` in handler.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays for realloc growth on append; receives refunds the program
chooses to issue (currently none — pages do not shrink on remove).

instructions.name: remove_controller
docs: Remove a controller address.
Symmetric to `add_controller` — `controller_acl_config` and
`controller_acl_page` (containing the live entry) are required,
so the ACL entry is removed in the same transaction as the
`AntControllers` mutation. Pages are not realloc-shrunk on
remove (see ADR-012 / `remove_acl_*_handler`); rent-recovery
happens lazily via `close_acl_page` / `close_acl_config`.

instructions.name: remove_record
docs: Remove a record (owner/controllers only). Cannot remove the @ record.

accounts.name: record
docs: Record account to close (rent returned to caller, who is an authorized owner/controller)

accounts.name: record_metadata
docs: Optional sibling AntRecordMetadata PDA. When the record being removed
has a metadata PDA, callers SHOULD pass it here so both close in the
same tx. If a caller forgets, the orphan is recoverable via the
permissionless `close_orphaned_record_metadata` instruction (rent
flows to whoever invokes the cleanup).
Pass the ario-ant program ID as the account key to signal `None`.

instructions.name: remove_record_metadata
docs: Remove metadata for a record. Owner/controllers only.
Rent is returned to the caller.

accounts.name: record_metadata
docs: Metadata PDA to close — rent returned to caller.

instructions.name: set_description
docs: Set ANT description.

instructions.name: set_keywords
docs: Set ANT keywords.

instructions.name: set_logo
docs: Set ANT logo.

instructions.name: set_name
docs: Set ANT display name.

instructions.name: set_record
docs: Set (create or update) a record for an undername.
New records: owner/controller only.
Existing records: owner/controller or record owner (without priority change).

instructions.name: set_record_metadata
docs: Set (create or update) metadata for a record's undername.
Metadata is stored in a separate PDA to save rent on records that
don't use display_name/logo/description/keywords.

accounts.name: record
docs: Core record — must already exist. Used for permission checks.

accounts.name: record_metadata
docs: Metadata PDA — created on first use, updated on subsequent calls.

instructions.name: set_ticker
docs: Set ANT ticker.

instructions.name: sync_attributes
docs: Sync the Metaplex Core Attributes plugin to reflect a single
ArnsRecord (`name`). Reads the canonical ario-arns record at
`arns_record` via the typed `ario_arns::state::ArnsRecord`,
validates `record.ant == asset.key()`, preserves any existing
`ANT Program` trait (so ADR-016 / BD-100 routing isn't silently
dropped), and CPIs `mpl_core::UpdatePluginV1` to overwrite the
plugin payload with `[ArNS Name, Type, Undername Limit (+ ANT
Program)]`.
Authority: ANTs mint with the Attributes plugin authority set to
`Owner` — only the current MPL Core asset holder can sign the
inner CPI. `authority` MUST equal the on-chain owner (Anchor
handler check) and is forwarded as both `payer` and `authority`
to the CPI.
Reshape rationale: pre-Sprint-3, ario-arns `buy_name` /
`upgrade_name` / etc. CPI'd into MPL Core themselves (and skipped
the CPI when the buyer wasn't the ANT holder, requiring a
permissionless re-converge ix). The reshape inverts that —
ario-arns no longer touches MPL, the SDK composes `arns.buy_name`
+ `ant.sync_attributes` in one tx, and this is the single point
where the Attributes plugin is written.

accounts.name: asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset whose Attributes plugin will be updated.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays for any rent the inner CPI may charge. Often the same key as
`authority` (ANT owner) but kept separate so a sponsor can pay.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal the asset's current MPL Core owner — the Attributes
plugin uses `Owner` as its `BasePluginAuthority`, so only the
holder can sign UpdatePluginV1.

accounts.name: arns_record
docs: PDA seeds + `ant == asset.key()` are all validated in the handler.

accounts.name: mpl_core_program
docs: Metaplex Core program — pinned to the canonical ID; required for
the inner CPI.

instructions.name: transfer
docs: Transfer the ANT to a new owner.
This is a wrapped MPL Core `transferV1` CPI that bundles in the
post-conditions our protocol needs:
1. CPI to MPL Core to actually move the asset.
2. Inline reconcile — clears `AntControllers` and updates
`AntConfig.last_known_owner = new_owner`.
3. Inline ACL swap — `record_owner(new_owner)` +
`remove_owner(old_owner)`.
Both ACL configs/pages are required accounts so it is not
possible to transfer through this path without keeping the
per-owner ACL view in sync. Ex-controllers' ACL entries are NOT
touched here (variable cardinality is incompatible with strong
Codama account typing); the SDK opportunistically bundles
`remove_acl_controller` for each, and the standalone healing ix
can be called permissionlessly to clean up later.
Direct MPL Core `transferV1` calls (e.g., from marketplaces that
do not know about ARIO) leave both `AntControllers` and the ACL
stale — the permissionless `reconcile` + `remove_acl_owner`
instructions exist exactly for that fallback path.

accounts.name: asset
docs: Metaplex Core asset being transferred (writable for the CPI).

accounts.name: caller
docs: Current asset owner — also the MPL Core authority and rent payer
for any new owner page realloc growth.

accounts.name: new_owner
docs: New owner of the asset. Pubkey-only; the actual ownership move
happens via the MPL Core CPI.
`new_owner_acl_config` seed binding (`AclConfig.user == this`)
and the post-CPI MPL Core state.

accounts.name: new_owner_acl_config
docs: New owner's `AclConfig`. Required so we can write
`record_owner(new_owner)` inline. SDK preflight bundles
`register_acl_config` first if it does not exist yet.

accounts.name: new_owner_acl_page
docs: New owner's destination `AclPage` (first non-full page chosen by
SDK). SDK preflight bundles `add_acl_page` if every existing
page is at `MAX_ACL_PAGE_ENTRIES`.

accounts.name: old_owner_acl_config
docs: Old owner's `AclConfig` (= caller). Required so we can
`swap_remove` the (asset, Owner) entry inline.

accounts.name: old_owner_acl_page
docs: Old owner's `AclPage` containing the live (asset, Owner) entry.
SDK looks this up via `getMultipleAccountsInfo` over
`[0..page_count)` before calling.

accounts.name: mpl_core_program
docs: MPL Core program — invoked via CPI to do the actual asset transfer.

instructions.name: transfer_record
docs: Transfer record ownership to another address.

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Unauthorized: caller is not the ANT owner or a controller

errors.name: UnauthorizedRecordAccess
errors.msg: Unauthorized: caller does not have permission for this record

errors.name: InvalidUndername
errors.msg: Invalid undername format

errors.name: UndernameTooLong
errors.msg: Undername too long (max 61 characters)

errors.name: InvalidTarget
errors.msg: Invalid target for the specified protocol

errors.name: UnsupportedProtocol
errors.msg: Unsupported target protocol

errors.name: TargetTooLong
errors.msg: Target too long (max 128 characters)

errors.name: InvalidTtl
errors.msg: TTL out of range (must be 60-86400 seconds)

errors.name: RecordNotFound
errors.msg: Record not found

errors.name: RecordHasNoOwner
errors.msg: Record has no owner, cannot transfer

errors.name: RecordTransferToSelf
errors.msg: Cannot transfer record to current owner

errors.name: ControllerAlreadyExists
errors.msg: Controller already exists

errors.name: ControllerNotFound
errors.msg: Controller not found

errors.name: MaxControllersReached
errors.msg: Maximum controllers reached (10)

errors.name: NameTooLong
errors.msg: Name too long (max 61 characters)

errors.name: NameEmpty
errors.msg: Name cannot be empty

errors.name: DescriptionTooLong
errors.msg: Description too long (max 256 characters)

errors.name: TooManyKeywords
errors.msg: Too many keywords (max 8)

errors.name: KeywordTooLong
errors.msg: Keyword too long (max 32 characters)

errors.name: InvalidKeyword
errors.msg: Invalid keyword format (alphanumeric, dash, underscore, #, @ only, no spaces)

errors.name: DuplicateKeyword
errors.msg: Duplicate keyword

errors.name: InvalidLogo
errors.msg: Logo must be a valid 43-character Arweave address

errors.name: CannotChangePriorityOfRoot
errors.msg: Cannot change priority of @ record (must be 0)

errors.name: PriorityRequiresOwnerOrController
errors.msg: Priority can only be set by owner or controllers

errors.name: OnlyOwnerOrControllerCanCreate
errors.msg: Only owner or controllers can create new records

errors.name: InvalidAsset
errors.msg: Invalid Metaplex Core asset

errors.name: NotNftHolder
errors.msg: Caller is not the NFT holder

errors.name: CannotRemoveRootRecord
errors.msg: Cannot remove the @ (root) record

errors.name: TickerTooLong
errors.msg: Ticker too long (max 16 characters)

errors.name: TickerEmpty
errors.msg: Ticker cannot be empty

errors.name: MigrationInactive
errors.msg: Migration is not active

errors.name: MigrationAlreadyFinalized
errors.msg: Migration has already been finalized

errors.name: InvalidAccountData
errors.msg: Invalid account data for migration import

errors.name: InvalidPda
errors.msg: PDA derivation does not match target account

errors.name: AlreadyLatestVersion
errors.msg: ANT is already at the latest version

errors.name: MigrationExpired
errors.msg: Migration deadline has passed

errors.name: RecordStillExists
errors.msg: Cannot close metadata: corresponding record still exists

errors.name: AclEntryAlreadyExists
errors.msg: ACL entry already exists for this (asset, role) pair

errors.name: AclEntryNotFound
errors.msg: ACL entry not found for this (asset, role) pair

errors.name: AclPageFull
errors.msg: ACL page has reached MAX_ACL_PAGE_ENTRIES — allocate a new page

errors.name: AclConfigNotEmpty
errors.msg: Cannot close ACL config: user still has allocated pages

errors.name: AclPageNotEmpty
errors.msg: Cannot close ACL page: page still has live entries

errors.name: AclPageNotLast
errors.msg: Only the most recently allocated page can be closed

errors.name: AclPageUserMismatch
errors.msg: Supplied AclPage does not belong to the target user

errors.name: AclPageIndexMismatch
errors.msg: Supplied AclPage index does not match the seeds used to derive it

errors.name: AclPageOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Page index out of bounds for AclConfig.page_count

errors.name: AclConfigUserMismatch
errors.msg: AclConfig.user does not match the target user address

errors.name: AclUnknownRole
errors.msg: Unknown AclRole codepoint — see AclRole::try_from_u8

errors.name: NotCurrentOwner
errors.msg: Target address is not the current owner of this asset

errors.name: NotCurrentController
errors.msg: Target address is not currently a controller of this asset

errors.name: StillCurrentOwner
errors.msg: Cannot remove ACL entry: address is still the current owner

errors.name: StillCurrentController
errors.msg: Cannot remove ACL entry: address is still a current controller

errors.name: TransferToSelf
errors.msg: Cannot transfer asset to its current owner

errors.name: InvalidArnsRecord
errors.msg: Invalid ArnsRecord for sync_attributes

types.name: AclConfig
docs: Head record for a user's paginated ACL (ADR-012).
Stores the page count + total entry count. SDK reads this once, then
fans out to each `AclPage` via `getMultipleAccountsInfo`. There is no
protocol-level cap on how many pages a user can have — `page_count` is
`u64` so the design carries no implicit ceiling.
Seeds: `["acl_config", user.as_ref()]`

fields.name: user
docs: The wallet this ACL belongs to.

fields.name: page_count
docs: Number of `AclPage` PDAs allocated for this user.
Pages are addressed by their index `[0, page_count)`.

fields.name: total_entries
docs: Total live entries across all pages. Maintained by the program on
every record/remove ix; lets clients sanity-check fan-out reads.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: AclEntry
docs: Single ACL entry: relationship between `user` (encoded in the parent
PDA seeds) and a Metaplex Core asset.
Layout: `Pubkey (32) + u8 (1) = 33 bytes`. See `MAX_ACL_PAGE_ENTRIES`.

fields.name: asset
docs: MPL Core asset (ANT mint) this relationship references.

fields.name: role
docs: Relationship kind — see `AclRole`. Stored as raw `u8` so future
roles slot in without a struct change.

types.name: AclEntryAddedEvent
docs: Emitted when an ACL entry is added (Owner via `record_acl_owner`,
Controller via `record_acl_controller`). The `role` discriminator
(`ACL_ROLE_*`) distinguishes the two entry types.

fields.name: role
docs: 0 = Owner, 1 = Controller

types.name: AclEntryRemovedEvent
docs: Emitted when an ACL entry is removed (Owner via `remove_acl_owner`,
Controller via `remove_acl_controller`).

fields.name: role
docs: 0 = Owner, 1 = Controller

types.name: AclPage
docs: One page of ACL entries for a user (ADR-012).
Pages are independent PDAs deterministically addressed by their index,
so any individual page can be fetched in O(1) without scanning sibling
pages. Removal uses `Vec::swap_remove` inside the page; the SDK's
"append to first non-full page" rule consumes any holes before
allocating a new page, so density is preserved at realistic churn
without cross-page bookkeeping.
Seeds: `["acl_page", user.as_ref(), &page_idx.to_le_bytes()]`
(`page_idx.to_le_bytes()` is always 8 bytes — `u64` LE encoding).

fields.name: user
docs: Owning user (matches `AclConfig.user`).

fields.name: page_idx
docs: Index of this page within the user's ACL.

fields.name: entries
docs: Live entries on this page. Length ≤ `MAX_ACL_PAGE_ENTRIES`.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: AntConfig
docs: Per-ANT configuration and metadata stored alongside the Metaplex Core asset.
Seeds: ["ant_config", mint]

fields.name: mint
docs: The Metaplex Core asset (NFT mint) this config belongs to

fields.name: name
docs: ANT display name (max 61 chars, matches Lua Name field)

fields.name: ticker
docs: Ticker symbol (e.g. "ANT")

fields.name: logo
docs: Logo - Arweave transaction ID (43 chars)

fields.name: description
docs: Description (max 256 chars)

fields.name: keywords
docs: Keywords (max 8, each max 32 chars)

fields.name: last_known_owner
docs: Last known NFT owner - for lazy controller cleanup on transfer

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version — used for per-ANT data migrations

types.name: AntControllers
docs: Controller list for an ANT.
Seeds: ["ant_controllers", mint]

fields.name: mint
docs: The Metaplex Core asset this controller list belongs to

fields.name: controllers
docs: Controller addresses (max 10)

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version — see `ANT_CONTROLLERS_VERSION`. Forward-compat marker
for future schema migrations; production handlers don't currently
branch on it.

types.name: AntMetadataUpdatedEvent
docs: Emitted by every `set_*` ANT-config metadata setter
(`set_name`, `set_ticker`, `set_description`, `set_keywords`,
`set_logo`). The `field` discriminator (see `ANT_METADATA_FIELD_*`)
tells consumers which field changed without bloating the IDL with a
String value field. `new_value` carries the new string.

fields.name: field
docs: 0 = name, 1 = ticker, 2 = description, 3 = keywords, 4 = logo

types.name: AntMigrationConfig
docs: Migration configuration for the ANT program.
Separate from AntConfig because AntConfig is per-NFT.
PDA: ["ant_migration_config"]

fields.name: authority
docs: Admin authority (multi-sig)

fields.name: migration_authority
docs: Dedicated migration authority (hot key for batch imports)

fields.name: migration_active
docs: Whether migration import is active (permanently disabled by finalize_migration)

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

types.name: AntReconciledEvent
docs: Emitted by `reconcile` when ownership has actually changed since the
last reconcile. No-op reconciles (the asset hasn't moved) do NOT
emit — keeps the indexer activity feed clean. `controllers_cleared`
is `true` whenever `reconcile` ran and the controller list was
cleared (always true on a real ownership change in the current
implementation; reserved as a discriminator in case future versions
allow controller retention across transfers).

types.name: AntRecord
docs: A single undername record for an ANT (core fields only).
Optional per-record metadata lives in a separate AntRecordMetadata PDA
to avoid paying rent for rarely-used fields on every undername.
Seeds: ["ant_record", mint, undername_hash]

fields.name: mint
docs: The Metaplex Core asset this record belongs to

fields.name: undername
docs: The undername (e.g. "@", "blog", "docs") — lowercase

fields.name: target
docs: Content address — Arweave TX ID (43 chars base64url), IPFS CID, or future protocol

fields.name: target_protocol
docs: Storage protocol (0 = arweave, 1 = ipfs, 2+ = reserved for future)

fields.name: ttl_seconds
docs: TTL in seconds (60-86400)

fields.name: priority
docs: Priority for ordering (None = unset, Some(0) required for "@")

fields.name: owner
docs: Optional record owner (delegated control)

fields.name: last_reconciled_owner
docs: Last reconciled owner — tracks the ANT owner at last record modification.
If this differs from config.last_known_owner, the record's `owner` is cleared
to prevent stale record-level ownership surviving NFT transfers.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version — see `ANT_RECORD_VERSION`.

types.name: AntRecordMetadata
docs: Optional per-record metadata, stored in a separate PDA to save rent on
the vast majority of records that don't use metadata fields.
Seeds: ["ant_record_meta", mint, undername_hash]

fields.name: mint
docs: The Metaplex Core asset this metadata belongs to (for getProgramAccounts filtering)

fields.name: undername_hash
docs: Hash of the undername (for PDA re-derivation in close/remove operations)

fields.name: display_name
docs: Optional display name (max 61 chars)

fields.name: record_logo
docs: Optional logo (Arweave TX ID, 43 chars — Arweave-only for permanence)

fields.name: record_description
docs: Optional description (max 256 chars)

fields.name: record_keywords
docs: Optional keywords (max 8, each max 32 chars)

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version — see `ANT_RECORD_METADATA_VERSION`.

types.name: AntTransferredEvent
docs: Emitted by the wrapped `transfer` instruction (MPL Core
`transferV1` CPI + reconcile + ACL swap). This is the asset-side
transfer event marketplaces / indexers subscribe to for ANT NFT
movement; it is NOT emitted on direct MPL Core transfers that
bypass our wrapper (see `reconcile` for the fallback path).

types.name: AttributesClearedEvent
docs: Emitted by `clear_attributes` after a successful UpdatePluginV1 CPI
that resets the asset's ArNS-related traits. The `ANT Program`
routing trait is preserved across the clear (BD-100); indexers use
this event to invalidate cached trait state when the on-chain
ArnsRecord no longer matches the asset (post-release / post-reassign).
`kept_program` is true if the asset had an `ANT Program` trait that
survived the clear, false if the post-clear attribute list is empty.

types.name: AttributesSyncedEvent
docs: Emitted by `sync_attributes` after a successful UpdatePluginV1 CPI.
Permissionless cross-program sync between ario-arns and the MPL
Core Attributes plugin — indexers use this to know when an ANT's
on-chain traits have been refreshed without polling the asset.

types.name: ControllerAddedEvent
docs: Emitted when a controller is added via `add_controller`.

types.name: ControllerRemovedEvent
docs: Emitted when a controller is removed via `remove_controller`.

fields.name: name
docs: ANT display name

fields.name: ticker
docs: Optional ticker (defaults to "ANT")

fields.name: target
docs: Content target for the @ record (Arweave TX ID, IPFS CID, etc.)

fields.name: target_protocol
docs: Target protocol (0 = Arweave, 1 = IPFS). Defaults to Arweave if not specified.

fields.name: logo
docs: Logo (Arweave TX ID, 43 chars) — empty for default

fields.name: description
docs: Description (max 256 chars)

fields.name: keywords
docs: Keywords (max 8)

types.name: RecordMetadataPrunedEvent
docs: Emitted by the permissionless `close_orphaned_record_metadata`
cleanup. `pruner` is the (possibly third-party) caller that
reclaimed the rent.

types.name: RecordMetadataRemovedEvent
docs: Emitted by `remove_record_metadata` when the metadata PDA is
closed and rent flows to the caller.

types.name: RecordMetadataUpdatedEvent
docs: Emitted by `set_record_metadata`. Record metadata is the optional
per-record sibling PDA (`AntRecordMetadata`) carrying display
name / logo / description / keywords. `field` is reserved for
future granular setters; `set_record_metadata` writes every field
in one ix and emits with `field = RECORD_METADATA_FIELD_ALL`.

types.name: RecordRemovedEvent
docs: Emitted when a record is deleted via `remove_record`.

types.name: RecordSetEvent
docs: Emitted when a record is created or updated via `set_record`.

types.name: RecordTransferredEvent
docs: Emitted when a record's `owner` is rotated via `transfer_record`.
`previous_owner` is `None` when the record had no prior assigned
owner (the typical case for first-time owner assignment).

fields.name: undername
docs: Undername (needed for PDA seed derivation)

fields.name: display_name
docs: Optional display name (max 61 chars)

fields.name: record_logo
docs: Optional logo (Arweave TX ID, 43 chars)

fields.name: record_description
docs: Optional description (max 256 chars)

fields.name: record_keywords
docs: Optional keywords (max 8)

fields.name: undername
docs: Undername (e.g. "@", "blog", "docs")

fields.name: target
docs: Content target (Arweave TX ID, IPFS CID, etc., max 128 chars)

fields.name: target_protocol
docs: Target protocol (0 = Arweave, 1 = IPFS)

fields.name: ttl_seconds
docs: TTL in seconds (60-86400)

fields.name: priority
docs: Priority (only owner/controllers can set)

fields.name: record_owner
docs: Record owner address (only owner/controllers can assign)
